humanities_role_explanation: HistBench contains questions evaluating both factual historical knowledge (content) and specialized, domain-specific methodological workflows of historical research, such as paleography, interdisciplinary synthesis, and source processing (domain).
benchmark_modalities_explanation: A multimodal benchmark combining textual questions with visual sources (manuscripts, maps, illustrations, charts, inscriptions), audio recordings (oral history), and video clips.
analytical_tasks_explanation: The benchmark operationalizes historical inquiry through bibliographic retrieval, source identification from non-textual formats, causal inference on historical events, interdisciplinary synthesis (such as archaeology and linguistics), cultural context decoding, and translation of classical or rare scripts.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specifically designed as a domain-specific evaluation suite targeting historical reasoning and historical research capabilities.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates several LLMs including GPT-4o, DeepSeek-R1, o3, o4-mini-high, and Grok 3.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Generative LLMs and LLM-based agent systems are the primary systems evaluated on the tasks.

arts_humanities_data.note: Features data relevant to History, Language and Linguistics, Archaeology, Classics, Religious Studies, and Visual Arts.

quantitative_metrics.note: Evaluates models using accuracy (pass@1, pass@2, and pass@3).

english_language.note: The paper is written in English, although the benchmark's primary sources span 29 different languages.

summary: This paper introduces HistBench, a comprehensive benchmark consisting of 414 expert-curated, multi-modal, and multi-lingual questions spanning 29 languages, designed to evaluate historical reasoning in AI. To address the poor performance of generalist LLMs on this benchmark, the authors introduce HistAgent, a domain-specialized agent framework equipped with targeted tools like historical OCR, translation, and literature search.
key_findings: Generalist LLMs and search agents fail on historical reasoning due to OCR limits, poor translation of minor/dead languages, and lack of expert search tools. On HistBench, HistAgent based on GPT-4o achieves 27.54% pass@1 and 36.47% pass@2, outperforming general GPT-4o (18.60%), DeepSeek-R1 (14.49%), and Open Deep Research (25.12% pass@2). Furthermore, HistAgent maintains generalist reasoning with 60.00% pass@1 accuracy on GAIA.
